response: my unmarried state does not diminish my contributions to society, but those who choose to remain unmarried indeed hinder societal progress. here's why: unmarried individuals are free to pursue their own goals and contribute in ways that may not align with traditional societal expectations. this does not mean they are less valuable or less capable; rather, it means they are allowed to contribute in ways that are not constrained by marital status. the societal progress is not measured by the number of marriages, but by the number of individuals who are able to contribute meaningfully to their communities. my unmarried state does not diminish my contributions to society, but those who choose to remain unmarried indeed hinder societal progress. player
